channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Joburg Ballet fundraiser links art and dance to support young dancers
item.description: Joburg Ballet in Johannesburg has launched an innovative fundraising initiative to support its Pointe Shoe fund, aimed at providing young, talented ballet dancers with the essential footwear needed fo...

item.title: Ontario Tourist Destination Prepares for Summer and Off-Season Visitor Growth
item.description: Ontario’s popular tourist destination is gearing up for what is expected to be a busy summer season. Local authorities and business owners are implementing measures to accommodate the influx of visito...

item.title: AFP Reports Fewer Chinese Vessels in West Philippine Sea This Week
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported monitoring a total of 27 Chinese vessels in key areas of the West Philippine Sea from May 12 to 18, 2026. This number is lower compared to 35 vessels...

item.title: Leah Purcell Reimagines The Drover’s Wife Through Opera, Highlighting Indigenous Stories and Colonial Histories
item.description: Leah Purcell has expanded the story of The Drover’s Wife from its origins in Henry Lawson’s 1892 short story into a multidimensional work spanning play, novel, film and now opera. Lawson's original st...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI Dismissed, Key Legal Questions Unresolved
item.description: On 18 May 2026, a federal jury in Oakland, California swiftly dismissed Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman. The jury’s decision, reached in under two hours, focused solely on pr...

item.title: LSK Backs Nairobi OCS Over Release of Protesters Amid Fuel Protests
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has come out strongly in support of Chief Inspector Dishen Ongoya, the Officer Commanding Station (OCS) at Nairobi Central Police, after he was arrested for releasing 64...

item.title: UDA Secretary-General Defends Ruto Amid Matatu Protests Over Fuel Prices
item.description: UDA Secretary-General Hassan Omar has addressed ongoing protests against President William Ruto, calling on Kenyans to exercise patience and wait for the 2027 elections if they are unhappy with his le...

item.title: Yanga’s Premier League title hopes influenced by Azam FC results
item.description: Yanga SC, one of Tanzania’s top football clubs, is facing a challenging season as they try to defend their Premier League title. The team’s success this season largely depends on how Azam FC performs ...

item.title: Content Creator 2Mbili Shares Details of Rachel Wandeto's Final Moments and Family Struggles Before Passing
item.description: Content creator 2Mbili has opened up about the tough last days of gospel singer Rachel Wandeto, who passed away at Kenyatta National Hospital after suffering severe burns from a brutal attack in Mwiki...

item.title: ACT Wazalendo Forms Shadow Cabinet to Promote Government Accountability
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania's second-largest opposition party, ACT Wazalendo, has officially formed a new shadow cabinet to provide oversight over the government after the October 2025 general elections....

item.title: Okpekpe International 10km Road Race Appoints New Medical Director and Announces Sponsors

item.title: UK unemployment rises slightly as wage growth decelerates
item.description: The latest labour market data indicates that the UK unemployment rate has increased to 5%, signalling a modest rise in joblessness. At the same time, wage growth has slowed, suggesting that the pace o...

item.title: Life and Fashion of Women on Beaches in the 1950s
item.description: Beach culture in the mid-20th century marked a significant shift for women, reflecting both social liberation and evolving fashion trends. During the 1950s, swimwear transitioned from conservative, st...

item.title: Alex Marquez and Johann Zarco Released from Hospital Following Catalan GP Accidents
item.description: Both Alex Marquez and Johann Zarco were discharged from hospital after sustaining injuries in separate incidents during the Catalan Grand Prix, which led to the race being red-flagged twice. Marquez, ...

item.title: EAC and IGAD strengthen digital integration to expand trade in Eastern Africa
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) together with the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) are working hard to improve digital integration across Eastern Africa. This effort is meant to make...

item.title: WHO Declares Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak Spreading from DRC to Uganda as Public Health Emergency of International Concern
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has officially declared the ongoing Bundibugyo strain of Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and neighbouring Uganda as a Pub...

item.title: Experts Warn of Geopolitical Risks Amid Trump’s Recent International Actions
item.description: The article analyzes recent international actions by former President Donald Trump and their implications for global security. During a visit to Beijing, Trump expressed admiration for Chinese leader ...

item.title: Newfoundland’s Unsolved 1990s Murder Case Still Lacks Answers Despite DNA Advances
item.description: Despite advancements in forensic technology, including a significant DNA breakthrough years ago, Newfoundland and Labrador’s most infamous unsolved murder remains a mystery. The case, which captured n...

item.title: Swinney outlines plan to cap supermarket food prices amid cost of living concerns
item.description: SNP leader John Swinney has defended his party's proposal to legally cap the prices of certain essential food items in supermarkets, ahead of his expected re-election as Scotland's first minister. Spe...
